title: aparine tricornis
intro: Aparine tricornis, also known as three-horned dodder or three-horned sticky snakeroot, is a perennial plant with a sticky, glue-like substance on its stems that helps it to attach to other plants for support. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Aparine tricornis include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and treating various infections. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and can be used to treat conditions such as fever, sore throats, and skin conditions.
